title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Authorization, Ethics, Privacy, and Evidence · Program Brief: Rules Legal Review
summary: A team says “scrape this public site” without a site owner, permitted purpose, data policy, stop contact, accessibility outcome, or proof that the result helps anyone. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the rules legal review increment in authorized stakeholder and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Separate technical policy from terms, contracts, copyright, privacy, and jurisdiction questions and route uncertain cases to qualified review.
Record who controls the target, what access is authorized, which identities and paths are permitted, and who can pause or revoke the crawl.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-rules-legal-review — Separate technical policy from terms, contracts, copyright, privacy, and jurisdiction questions and route uncertain cases to qualified review.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-outcomes-authorization-evidence-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-outcomes-authorization-evidence-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_outcomes_authorization_evidence_4(authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 8, minimized_fields: int = 6, retained_fields: int = 6, transfer_gates_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "authorization-missing"
    if acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "outcome-underdefined"
    if minimized_fields < 1 or retained_fields != minimized_fields:
        return False, retained_fields, "data-minimization"
    if not transfer_gates_named:
        return False, retained_fields, "evidence-overclaim"
    assert authorized and acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "authorized-crawl-charter"

options.text: For repair the rules legal review defect, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. Keep the Rules Legal Review artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the rules legal review defect evidence boundary for Rules Legal Review.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Rules Legal Review model · Theory Rules Legal Review
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rules Legal Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 9540 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the crawl charter on authorization, stakeholder value, minimized data, and honest evidence limits; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate technical policy from terms, contracts, copyright, privacy, and jurisdiction questions and route uncertain cases to qualified review.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Rules Legal Review model · Theory Rules Legal Review.
steps.why: Retrieving Rules Legal Review through the concrete authorized stakeholder and evidence charter case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Rules Legal Review model · Theory Rules Legal Review, keep Rules Legal Review connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate technical policy from terms, contracts, copyright, privacy, and jurisdiction questions and route uncertain cases to qualified review.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Rules Legal Review model · Theory Rules Legal Review, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Rules Legal Review precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Rules Legal Review model · Theory Rules Legal Review, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 9540 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the crawl charter on authorization, stakeholder value, minimized data, and honest evidence limits; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate technical policy from terms, contracts, copyright, privacy, and jurisdiction questions and route uncertain cases to qualified review.

For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Rules Legal Review model · Theory Rules Legal Review, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: robots.txt is a universal legal permission document.
